headline: France's film funding agency targeted by the far right
description: Without the National Center for Cinema and the Moving Image, many French films could never be made. But the CNC is now under attack, which could spell the end of this nearly 80-year-old institution if the far-right Rassemblement National comes to power.

headline: US says Iran deal still possible, as Trump tempers expectations
Senegalese president fires longtime ally Ousmane Sonko, plunging country into political instability
Authorities investigate safety lapses after China coal mine blast kills at least 82
description: Rescuers continued to search on Sunday for missing people following the country's deadliest coal mine explosion in recent years. Local officials reported that the coal mine located in the northern city of Changzhi had 'seriously' violated the law, but did not elaborate.

headline: Essayist Paul Klotz: With the Enhanced Games, 'we are witnessing the return of the circus'
description: By pitting doped athletes against each other under medical supervision, the Enhanced Games, funded by leading figures in American transhumanism, mark the beginning of a human modification economy.

headline: Djokovic fights through tough Roland Garros opener
description: The 39-year-old Novak Djokovic battled the big-serving Frenchman Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard as the clay-court Grand Slam got underway on Sunday.

headline: A €100,000 contract and possible mistaken identity: New clues emerge in murder of anti-drug activist's brother
description: The case file shows that the Marseille murder stemmed from major organized crime, involving several competing teams of hitmen. According to some suspects' statements, it was Amine Kessaci and not his brother Mehdi who was targeted, due to his commitment to fighting drug trafficking.
byline: Grégoire Biseau,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

Why dropping the worst-case climate scenario does not undermine the reality of global warming
description: A new generation of climate scenarios has set aside RCP8.5, which projected a nearly 5°C rise in global temperatures by 2100. Donald Trump presents this as proof of 'alarmism' among climate scientists, but the new projections remain highly concerning.

headline: Five arrested over smuggling of Californian cannabis to France
description: A former influencer known as 'PA7' and a counterfeiter known as 'the Prince of Fake Luxury Watches' are suspected of having illegally imported hundreds of kilograms of 'Cali' marijuana.

headline: Cannes: 'Everytime' by Sandra Wollner picks up the Un Certain Regard Prize
description: The competition of 18 feature films was marked by stories exploring the maternal bond and the disappearance of young girls.

headline: 'The most popular Palestinian leader has been imprisoned by Israel since 2002'
